# Crash-recovery failpoint matrix (Task 4.11)

Generated from the machine-readable `stateTable` in [`recovery-state-machine.md`](../recovery-state-machine.md).
Every progression checkpoint contributes a crash BEFORE and AFTER its atomic write; every terminal
(base + `failed@`/`cancelled@` suffixed) contributes a mid-write crash. 25 failpoints.

| Failpoint | State | Kind | Phase | Audit | Retained | Expected recovery |
|-----------|-------|------|-------|-------|----------|-------------------|
| `agent-committed@after-write` | agent-committed | checkpoint | after-write | — | 3 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (commitSha exists on agent branch and its tree hashes to treeHash) and advances to one of [integrated, failed@agent-committed, cancelled@agent-committed] per: commit present: integrate directly (v2 #335: no Tier-3 review park); commit missing but worktree present: recover as worktree-applied |
| `agent-committed@before-write` | agent-committed | checkpoint | before-write | — | 3 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: commitSha exists on agent branch and its tree hashes to treeHash |
| `cancelled@agent-committed@terminal-write` | cancelled@agent-committed | terminal | terminal-write | run.cancelled | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean any orphaned worktree |
| `cancelled@patched@terminal-write` | cancelled@patched | terminal | terminal-write | run.cancelled | 4 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash); no worktree to clean |
| `cancelled@planned@terminal-write` | cancelled@planned | terminal | terminal-write | run.cancelled | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash); no worktree to clean |
| `cancelled@terminal-write` | cancelled | terminal | terminal-write | run.cancelled | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean any orphaned worktree |
| `cancelled@worktree-applied@terminal-write` | cancelled@worktree-applied | terminal | terminal-write | run.cancelled | 4 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean the orphaned worktree |
| `failed@agent-committed@terminal-write` | failed@agent-committed | terminal | terminal-write | run.failed | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean any orphaned worktree |
| `failed@patched@terminal-write` | failed@patched | terminal | terminal-write | run.failed | 4 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash); no worktree to clean |
| `failed@planned@terminal-write` | failed@planned | terminal | terminal-write | run.failed | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash); no worktree to clean |
| `failed@terminal-write` | failed | terminal | terminal-write | run.failed | 3 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean any orphaned worktree |
| `failed@worktree-applied@terminal-write` | failed@worktree-applied | terminal | terminal-write | run.failed | 4 | terminal — re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and clean the orphaned worktree |
| `finalized@terminal-write` | finalized | terminal | terminal-write | run.integrated | 4 | none — run is done; reconciler treats as a no-op. Rollback of a finalized run is a separate operator-initiated run linked by rollbackOf, not a transition out of finalized |
| `integrated@after-write` | integrated | checkpoint | after-write | run.integrated | 3 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (canonical ref contains canonicalSha AND audit_intents row for (runId, seq) exists with matching payloadHash) and advances to one of [reindexed] per: per-step, keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash): (a) canonical advanced but no intent -> allocate seq/payloadHash, write intent, append, step-3 CAS; (b) intent present, ref lacks (runId, seq) -> append then step-3 CAS; (c) ref has event, state still agent-committed -> step-3 CAS; (d) state already integrated -> advance to reindexed/finalized idempotently on (runId, seq) |
| `integrated@before-write` | integrated | checkpoint | before-write | run.integrated | 3 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: canonical ref contains canonicalSha AND audit_intents row for (runId, seq) exists with matching payloadHash |
| `patched@after-write` | patched | checkpoint | after-write | — | 3 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (state='patched' and patchHash matches recompute from stored plan) and advances to one of [worktree-applied, failed@patched, cancelled@patched] per: recompute patches from plan; match patchHash advance, else failed@patched (reason patch-nondeterministic) |
| `patched@before-write` | patched | checkpoint | before-write | — | 3 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: state='patched' and patchHash matches recompute from stored plan |
| `planned@after-write` | planned | checkpoint | after-write | run.planned | 1 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (row state='planned' and stored planHash matches recompute) and advances to one of [patched, finalized, failed@planned, cancelled@planned] per: no side effects past the row; advance to patched if inputs unchanged else failed@planned (reason plan-stale) |
| `planned@before-write` | planned | checkpoint | before-write | run.planned | 1 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: row state='planned' and stored planHash matches recompute |
| `reindexed@after-write` | reindexed | checkpoint | after-write | — | 3 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (projection reflects canonicalSha at indexGeneration) and advances to one of [finalized] per: re-run the idempotent projection step (derived state is rebuildable from canonical Markdown), then advance to finalized |
| `reindexed@before-write` | reindexed | checkpoint | before-write | — | 3 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: projection reflects canonicalSha at indexGeneration |
| `rejected@terminal-write` | rejected | terminal | terminal-write | run.rejected | 3 | re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) (intent present -> skip step 1; ref carries (runId, seq) -> skip step 2; then step-3 CAS); nothing to roll forward |
| `rolled-back@terminal-write` | rolled-back | terminal | terminal-write | run.rolled_back | 4 | operator-initiated only; on crash mid-rollback re-run the §2.8 protocol from the first incomplete step keyed on (runId, seq, payloadHash) and re-drive the projection; never auto-initiated by the reconciler |
| `worktree-applied@after-write` | worktree-applied | checkpoint | after-write | — | 3 | checkpoint durable — recovery detects it (worktree exists at worktreePath hashing to treeHash) and advances to one of [agent-committed, failed@worktree-applied, cancelled@worktree-applied] per: applied-uncommitted: commit iff planHash+baseRef unmoved, else failed@worktree-applied (reason base-moved); orphaned worktree with no live run: clean |
| `worktree-applied@before-write` | worktree-applied | checkpoint | before-write | — | 3 | no durable write yet — recovery re-derives and idempotently retries the atomic write, keyed on: worktree exists at worktreePath hashing to treeHash |
